question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the silent RetryException catch block (catch (RetryException e) { return systemFile.genesisFile(); }) to cause the system to serve genesis ThrottleDefinitions during a partition, where genesis throttle limits are higher than current production limits, allowing the attacker to submit transactions at rates that would normally be throttled?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the systemFile cache (expireAfterWrite=10m) and the exchangeRate cache (expireAfterWrite=60m) have different TTLs, so that during a network partition, the throttle definitions expire and fall back to genesis (after 10 min) while exchange rates remain cached (for 60 min), creating a window where throttle limits are genesis-era permissive but fees are still current, enabling high-volume attacks at genesis throttle rates?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of the systemFiles map (via @Getter(lazy=true)) so that if the first call to getSystemFiles() occurs during a network partition, the map is built with genesis fallbacks, and since Lombok's lazy getter uses double-checked locking, the genesis-populated map is permanently stored and never re-initialized, causing all subsequent requests to use genesis system files for the process lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, load() for the hapiPermissionFile returns null (since it has Bytes.EMPTY as genesis content and codec=null), causing downstream permission checks to fail open or closed depending on how null is handled, potentially allowing unauthorized operations or denying all operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in fileDataRepository.getFileAtTimestamp() throwing a runtime exception (not InvalidFileException), which is NOT caught by the retryTemplate predicate (predicate: e instanceof InvalidFileException), causing the exception to propagate out of loadWithRetry() uncaught, bypassing the genesis fallback and causing an unhandled exception in the EVM execution path?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the roundDownToHour() timestamp normalization to cause cache collisions during a network partition, where requests with timestamps spanning an hour boundary (e.g., 59:59 and 00:01 of the next hour) map to different CacheKeys, causing the post-partition recovery to be delayed by up to one full hour for requests in the previous hour bucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, the AtomicInteger attempt counter in loadWithRetry() increments on each ParseException but the nanoSeconds AtomicLong is set to fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1, and if the DB returns the same corrupt record repeatedly (e.g., due to a caching layer in front of the DB), all 9 retries consume the same corrupt record, exhausting retries without making progress and always falling back to genesis?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-phase cache lookup in load() (cache.get() then loadFromDB() then cache.put()) during a network partition to create a TOCTOU race condition where two concurrent threads both find a cache miss, both call loadFromDB() (which returns genesis fallback), and both call cache.put() with genesis data, permanently poisoning the cache with genesis data even after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition during the loadAll() initialization to cause the Collectors.toMap() call to fail if any system file entity ID resolves to a duplicate FileID, causing an IllegalStateException that prevents the systemFiles map from being initialized and making isSystemFile() always return false, causing all system file requests to be routed through the non-system file path?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, load() for the exchangeRateFileId returns the genesis ExchangeRateSet (via loadWithRetry fallback), which is then cached under the hour-rounded CacheKey, and after partition recovery, the cached genesis exchange rate continues to be served for up to 60 minutes, causing all fee calculations to use genesis-era HBAR/USD rates that may be orders of magnitude different from current rates?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadWithRetry() has no timeout on the overall retry loop (only maxRetries=9 with no delay between retries), causing 10 sequential DB calls to execute as fast as possible during a partition, creating a tight retry storm that saturates DB connection resources faster than a rate-limited retry with backoff would?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any staleness detection in load() so that after a network partition heals and the DB is updated with new exchange rates, the cached genesis/stale exchange rate continues to be served until the 60-minute TTL expires, creating a prolonged window where the attacker can exploit the known stale rate to underpay fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createMockAddressBook()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createMockAddressBook() uses CommonProperties.getInstance().getShard() and getRealm() at construction time, and if a network partition causes CommonProperties to be initialized with incorrect shard/realm values, the mock address book will contain an AccountID with wrong shard/realm, causing address book validation failures in downstream EVM components?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, the genesis ThrottleDefinitions returned by loadWithRetry() may contain higher TPS limits than the current production throttle definitions, allowing the attacker to submit transactions at genesis-era rates that exceed current network capacity, causing resource exhaustion on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() uses two different CacheManager instances (exchangeRatesCacheManager for exchange/fee files, defaultSystemFileCacheManager for others) and during a network partition that affects only the Caffeine cache backend for one manager, cause inconsistent behavior where some system files are served from cache and others fall through to the partitioned DB, creating a mixed-state EVM execution environment?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retryTemplate in SystemFileLoader has no delay between retries (no backoff configured, unlike the AbstractParserProperties retry which has minBackoff=500ms), causing 10 immediate sequential DB calls per request during a partition, and by sending concurrent requests, create a thundering herd that prevents the DB from recovering?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, load() returns null for system files not found in the systemFiles map (loadFromDB returns null when getSystemFiles().get(fileId) is null), and if the systemFiles map was populated during a prior partition with an incomplete set of FileIDs, legitimate system file requests return null permanently, causing EVM execution failures?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the V0490FileSchema.genesisNetworkProperties(), genesisFeeSchedules(), genesisExchangeRatesBytes(), or genesisThrottleDefinitions() calls in loadAll() to fail (if they depend on external resources), causing loadAll() to throw an exception that prevents the systemFiles map from being initialized and making the entire SystemFileLoader non-functional?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CacheKey record uses FileID (a protobuf-generated object) for equality, and if a network partition causes FileID objects to be constructed with different default field values (e.g., shardNum=0 vs shardNum not set), cache lookups fail to find existing entries, causing repeated DB calls and cache pollution with duplicate entries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, the genesis CurrentAndNextFeeSchedule returned by loadWithRetry() for feeSchedulesFileId may have an expiry time in the past (genesis era), causing downstream fee schedule selection logic to always use the 'next' fee schedule instead of the 'current' one, resulting in incorrect fee calculations?
